visual_features.overall_style: Modern, corporate marketing page with strong brand emphasis on red, flat design with very light shadows, large pill-shaped CTAs, and a clean, minimally decorative layout. The look is trustworthy and functional rather than lifestyle/editorial.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by color (bold red vs neutral gray), typographic scale, and spatial grouping. The primary headline 'Boat Insurance in and around Crowley' in large red type is the dominant focal point. Secondary emphasis is on the right-hand CTA stack, especially the solid red 'Request a Quote' button. Tertiary elements include the town chips, section subheading 'Insure For Smooth Sailing', and the agent card. Body copy and gray chips are visually subdued using smaller font sizes and neutral colors.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous whitespace around major sections and between columns, with large top/bottom padding on the hero area and comfortable gaps between CTAs. Cards and chips have internal padding and are not tightly packed, giving a calm, readable rhythm. Margins between text blocks are moderate, preventing crowding while still keeping the page information-focused.
unique_elements: Large pill-shaped primary and secondary CTAs with strong red fill or outline
Pill-shaped gray chips listing service areas (town names) arranged in a responsive-looking grid
Right-hand sidebar panel with a highlighted question using a red left border accent inside a light gray container
Agent information card featuring a portrait, name, title, and circular icon buttons for contact methods
Top gray utility strip with language toggle and top red global nav bar combining logo, nav links, and phone number

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Two-column layout beneath a full-width top navigation, with a left main content column and a right sidebar containing CTAs and agent information. Content flows top-to-bottom within each column.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top utility bar (language link) → primary red navigation bar with logo, nav items, phone, login → hero/headline section with location chips and intro copy on the left, plus quote CTA card on the right → subsequent informational content and imagery beneath the hero in the left column → stacked CTA buttons and agent card continuing down the right sidebar.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Left-aligned text throughout main content and sidebar. The grid is center-aligned in the browser with consistent left and right content margins. Buttons and chips are horizontally centered within their own containers but aligned to a left column. Navigation links are horizontally aligned along a baseline in the top header.
responsive_hints: Two-column layout suggests collapsing to a single column on smaller screens, likely stacking the right sidebar content beneath the main content.
Pill-shaped location chips are arranged in a flexible row/wrap layout, indicating they would reflow into more rows on narrow viewports.
Navigation includes a dropdown label ('INSURANCE ▾'), which on mobile might convert to a hamburger or accordion style.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow within a two-column grid. The left column starts with a large hero headline, followed by a subtitle explaining coverage areas, then a grid of pill-shaped location chips in multiple rows, followed by a supporting subheading and body paragraph about coverage. Below that, a lifestyle image appears to illustrate boating. The right column starts at the same vertical level as the hero with a white card set on a subtle gray background that asks a question and visually frames the primary 'Request a Quote' CTA, followed by two large outline buttons ('File a Claim', 'Send Payment') stacked vertically, then an agent card with photo, name, title, and contact icons. Each block is clearly separated by consistent vertical spacing.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Approximately 60–65% of horizontal width is allocated to the left content column and 35–40% to the right sidebar. Vertically, the hero section (headline + sidebar quote card) occupies the upper ~40% of the visible page, followed by additional information and imagery which fill the remainder. Within the sidebar, the primary quote card and button occupy the top ~40% of the sidebar height above the fold, with secondary CTAs and agent card below. Individual components have generous padding (around 16–24px internally), and there are noticeable gaps (~24–32px) between stacked cards and buttons.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Medium-low density: the page leans spacious but is still information-focused. Headings and CTAs are clearly separated with ample breathing room, cards are not crowded, and each town chip has clear spacing around it. However, there is still a reasonable amount of text present, and multiple CTA options are visible at once, making it more functional than minimalist.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye first hits the red logo and top navigation (left to right), then is drawn down to the large red headline on the left. From there, the strong red 'Request a Quote' button in the right sidebar pulls attention across to the right. The user then scans down the left-side subheading and body copy, then continues downward to the boating image. Secondary attention flows down the right through the outline CTAs ('File a Claim', 'Send Payment') and finishes at the agent card. Overall, this creates an F-shaped scanning pattern: across the top nav, across the hero headline to the right CTA, then vertically down both columns.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary buttons are large pill-shaped rectangles with full rounding (border-radius likely 9999px or ~28–32px) and a solid primary red (#E2231A) background. Text is bold white, center-aligned, with generous horizontal padding (~32–40px) and vertical padding (~14–18px). No visible border; edges are smooth with flat, modern appearance and minimal or no shadow. Secondary buttons use the same pill shape and size but are white fill with a 2px red border and red label text. Buttons are stacked with consistent vertical spacing (~16–24px).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Semantically, primary actions should be <button> elements or <a> tags styled as buttons for navigation actions (e.g., 'Request a Quote' linking to a form). Markup pattern: <button class="btn btn-primary"> or <a class="btn btn-primary" href="..."> for primary; <button class="btn btn-outline"> for secondary. Icons, when present, would typically be inside <span> elements preceding the text. Buttons might live inside simple wrapper divs used for alignment and responsive behavior.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are white rectangles placed on a very light gray section background (#F6F6F6), giving them subtle contrast. they have slightly rounded corners (~4–8px radius), a thin light-gray border or soft drop shadow for separation, and ample internal padding (~20–24px). The quote prompt card includes a red vertical accent bar on the left of the text (a 3–4px wide stripe), with the text block to its right. The agent card contains a portrait image aligned left with a rounded rectangle or slightly rounded corners, text on the right, and a row of circular icon buttons underneath.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No full form is visible in the screenshot, but the pattern implied by the buttons and cards suggests flat, white input fields with subtle rounded corners (~4px). Inputs would likely have a 1px light gray border (#CCCCCC), inner padding (~10–12px), and on focus, a red border or outline. Labels would likely be above inputs, left-aligned, in a smaller, bold or medium-weight gray text. Error states would leverage the brand red for borders and messages; helper text in a smaller gray type below fields.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms would follow a stacked layout: <form> with <div class="form-group"> blocks containing <label for="id">Label</label><input id="id" .../> plus optional <small class="form-text">help</small>. Groups could be arranged into columns using the grid on larger screens and stacked on mobile. Validation messages would appear as sibling elements to inputs.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Top navigation is a horizontal red strip across the full width of the page. The logo is on the left, followed by navigation items ('INSURANCE', 'REVIEWS', 'JOBS', etc.), then phone number and 'LOG IN' on the right. Text is white and medium-weight; the active or hovered item may be underlined, use a darker or brighter red, or display a subtle bottom border. The 'INSURANCE' item includes a downward caret icon indicating a dropdown. Above this red bar, a thin light gray strip contains a right-aligned language toggle in red.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Location chips form a visually distinct list. Each item is a pill-shaped badge with a light gray background (#F6F6F6) and dark gray uppercase text. Items have equal horizontal padding (~16–20px) and vertical padding (~8–10px), and are spaced evenly with small gaps (~8–12px). There are no visible dividers; separation is achieved purely by spacing and shape. Another list-like pattern appears in the agent card with evenly spaced circular icon buttons in a row.

heading.visual_style: Bold, clean sans-serif that feels corporate and neutral, with relatively standard proportions and good legibility. The large red hero headline has a straightforward, slightly condensed appearance but not overly geometric.

body.visual_style: Neutral, readable sans-serif in regular weight, suitable for continuous text, with moderate x-height and smooth curves.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most buttons, chips, and cards use padding based on 8px multiples: buttons around 12–16px vertical and 24–32px horizontal; cards around 20–24px internal padding on all sides.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections appears to be around 32–48px, with slightly larger top/bottom padding for the hero area (48–64px).

interaction_hints: Primary buttons likely darken slightly or gain a subtle shadow on hover, with cursor: pointer and perhaps a smooth 150–200ms background-color transition.
Outline buttons probably invert on hover (red fill with white text) or increase border thickness, maintaining accessible contrast.
Navigation links likely underline or change shade on hover, with the active page having a persistent visual indicator (e.g., bolder weight or bottom border).
Location chips may gain a darker gray background or a subtle red outline on hover to indicate clickability.
Agent contact icons are likely circular buttons that change background color (e.g., from white to light gray or red) on hover, with icon color inversion.
Card hover states, if any, would be minimal—perhaps a light elevation (box-shadow) increase or border color shift to maintain a professional, non-flashy feel.
